sourceMovie.title: No Way Up
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Audiences latched onto No Way Up (2024) as a thriller theatrical experience: big enough for a group watch, specific enough to reward a second viewing once you know where it's headed. The thriller and horror blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Jaws
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If No Way Up clicked for you, Jaws (1975) is a strong next stop: both trade in thriller and horror, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: San Andreas
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If No Way Up clicked for you, San Andreas (2015) is a strong next stop: both trade in thriller and action, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after No Way Up?
faq.answer: Try Jaws, San Andreas, Man on Fire—each shares DNA with No Way Up while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is No Way Up?
faq.answer: No Way Up (2024) is best described as Thriller, Horror, Action. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is No Way Up worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.3/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this thriller lane.
